title: Pediatric CDS Review Record (canonical, ADR-0004)
description: Evidence Foundry E1 P1-T2 (FR-1/FR-2/FR-3), canonical replacement of the wave0 EP7-T1 shape at this exact file path -- ONE schema, not a parallel contract (ruling R5; full field-by-field mapping in .claude/worknotes/evidence-foundry-e1-v1/contracts-design.md §(a)). DATA CONTRACT ONLY: this repository does not yet ship any review-portal application, API, database, or server that produces or consumes these records. The canonical model is one IMMUTABLE, APPEND-ONLY file per review ACT -- one per role (`clinical-1`, `clinical-2`, `lab`, `adjudication`, `release-auth`) -- at `modules/<moduleId>/reviews/<review_id>.yaml` (OQ-2). There is no single mutable record whose state advances: corrections are new superseding records referencing the prior one via `supersedes`, never edits (ADR-0004 decision item 1). Whether a given proposal's full five-role chain has reached an authorizing outcome is a DERIVED, multi-file computation over which role-files exist for a `moduleId`/`subjectContentHash` pair (P2-T4's job), never a field stored on any one file here. D-4 (no synthetic/automated source may ever occupy a reviewer or approver position) is preserved as a three-layer system guarantee, not a single embedded-object const as in wave0: (1) `reviewerId` is a reference into `governance/reviewer-roster.yaml` (schemas/reviewer-roster.schema.json, P1-T4), where any `synthetic: false` entry requires an out-of-band `verificationRef`, a G1 human act; (2) this schema's own `synthetic` flag is downstream-rejected from ever satisfying a release-authorization validity check (P2-T4); (3) `scripts/validate-kb.mjs` (P1-T7) cross-checks `reviewerId` against the roster at `npm run validate` time -- a cross-file check this schema's own zero-dependency validator cannot express (no `$data` support). This schema alone cannot enforce humanness of a referenced `reviewerId` -- see the three-layer note above; do not read a structurally valid record here as proof a named human clinician reviewed anything, or as any clinical-validity, safety, or release-readiness claim. `signature` is schema-forced `null` on every non-synthetic (real) record below and stays that way until gates G1/G2 clear (see this schema's own `allOf`); on `synthetic: true` dry-run records it is a throwaway detached-Ed25519 test-key signature whose `keyId` carries the structural `TESTKEY-` marker, never a real signing key.

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Canonical review-record-shape version, reset to `1` -- this is a replacement of the wave0 shape lineage, not a value carried forward from it (contracts-design.md §a.2).

review_id.description: REQUIRED, non-null. OQ-2. Filename stem at `modules/<moduleId>/reviews/<review_id>.yaml`. `<seq4>` is a zero-padded 4-digit sequence number, global PER MODULE (not per role) -- so successive review acts for the same proposal share the module's sequence, not a per-role counter. The role suffix MUST agree with the `role` property (enforced below by this schema's own `allOf`).

role.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Explicit (not derived from `review_id`) so tooling never has to regex-parse the identifier. ADR-0004's five-role minimum-roles table. Cross-checked against `review_id`'s suffix below (this schema's own `allOf`, five branches, one per role).

moduleId.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Mirrors schemas/module-manifest.schema.json's `id` pattern exactly.

subjectContentHash.description: REQUIRED, non-null. OQ-2. SHA-256 of the exact target-artifact bytes this review act reviewed. Ties all five role-files for one proposal together (identical value across the set) without re-embedding proposal narrative into every review file (contracts-design.md §a.3 item 3).

previousRecordHash.description: REQUIRED key. OQ-2 hash-chain. `null` ONLY for the very first record ever created for a given `moduleId`. The chain itself -- that this value actually equals the prior record's own hash -- is enforced by the CLI/validator (P2-T3), not expressible as a pure schema constraint (needs the prior file's bytes); this schema only validates the shape of a present value.

supersedes.description: REQUIRED key. Non-null ONLY when this record is an explicit correction of a prior record referenced by its `review_id` -- corrections are new superseding files, never in-place edits (ADR-0004 decision item 1).

reviewerId.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Reference into `governance/reviewer-roster.yaml`'s `reviewerId` (schemas/reviewer-roster.schema.json, P1-T4). No inline `name`/`credential` on this schema -- identity facts live once, on the roster entry, not once per review file. This schema cannot itself verify the referenced id resolves to a human roster entry; see this schema's top-level description for the three-layer D-4 guarantee.

decision.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Narrowed from wave0's five-value per-reviewer enum (contracts-design.md §a.3 item 5e): `pending` is dropped because a canonical record is only ever created once a decision exists (no file means no act yet -- there is no mutable "pending" state to represent), and `abstain` is dropped because no ADR-0004 role or E1 functional requirement names abstention.

rationale.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Narrative justification for `decision` -- the REVIEWER's own reasoning for this review act, never a carry-over of any change-proposal author's rationale (a different fact, recorded elsewhere per contracts-design.md §a.3 item 3). Upgraded from wave0's optional `comment` to required (FR-28 audit posture: an append-only clinical review act always carries its reasoning).

reviewedAt.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Single timestamp for this immutable review act -- collapses wave0's separate `createdAt`/`updatedAt`/`decidedAt` triad, which is redundant once a record can never be edited after it is written (contracts-design.md §a.3 item 9).

synthetic.description: REQUIRED, non-null. OQ-2 / PRD FR-3 / FR-11. `true` ONLY for clearly-labeled dry-run records exercising this workflow before gates G1/G2 clear. A `synthetic: true` record is structurally incapable of satisfying any release-authorization validity check (P2-T4's job, downstream of this schema) regardless of its `decision` or `signature` -- being synthetic is not itself a defect, but this record can never stand in for a real human review act.

algorithm.description: Pinned to the single legal value `"ed25519"` -- the same ADR-0005 detached-signature mechanism used for release-manifest signing, computed over the canonicalized record bytes minus the signature object itself.

keyId.description: On a `synthetic: true` record this MUST carry the structural `TESTKEY-` prefix marking an ephemeral, never-committed-to-history test key (OQ-6). This `properties` shape only ever applies when `synthetic` is `true` -- see this schema's own `allOf` below, which forces the entire `signature` value to `null` whenever `synthetic` is `false`.

signature.description: REQUIRED key. OQ-2 signature-object shape. Schema-forced `null` on every `synthetic: false` (real) record below, mirroring `clinicalApprovers[]`'s/`approvedBy[]`'s `maxItems: 0` idiom for an object-typed field -- no real reviewer signature is ever accepted by this schema until gates G1 (named credentialed roster) and G2 (signing custodian + offline key ceremony) clear; raising this ceiling is a defect, not a feature. Git commit signatures are explicitly NOT this signature: the committer is the platform owner (author-in-effect), which would collapse signer and author onto the same identity and cannot express five distinct reviewer identities on the same commit.
